an4.94:1.1: “These four persons, *bhikkhus*, are found existing in the world.
an4.94:1.3: Here, *bhikkhus*, a certain person has attainment of inward settling of the mind [*ajjhattaṁ cetosamathassa*], but not attainment of insight into *dhammas* through higher discernment [*adhipaññādhammavipassanāya*].
an4.94:1.4: Here again, *bhikkhus*, a certain person has attainment of insight into *dhammas* through higher discernment [*adhipaññādhammavipassanāya*], but not attainment of inward settling of the mind [*ajjhattaṁ cetosamathassa*].
an4.94:1.5: Here again, *bhikkhus*, a certain person has neither attainment of inward settling of the mind [*ajjhattaṁ cetosamathassa*], nor attainment of insight into *dhammas* through higher discernment [*adhipaññādhammavipassanāya*].
an4.94:1.6: Here again, *bhikkhus*, a certain person has both attainment of inward settling of the mind [*ajjhattaṁ cetosamathassa*] and attainment of insight into *dhammas* through higher discernment [*adhipaññādhammavipassanāya*].
an4.94:2.1: There, *bhikkhus*, by the person who has attainment of inward settling of the mind [*ajjhattaṁ cetosamathassa*] but not attainment of insight into *dhammas* through higher discernment [*adhipaññādhammavipassanāya*], the person who has attainment of insight into *dhammas* through higher discernment [*adhipaññādhammavipassanāya*] should be approached, and he should be spoken to thus:
an4.94:2.4: How should *saṅkhāras* be inspected with insight [*vipassitabbā*]?’
an4.94:2.5: He answers him according to what he has seen and according to what he has understood:
an4.94:2.6: ‘In this way, friend, *saṅkhāras* should be seen; in this way *saṅkhāras* should be examined; in this way *saṅkhāras* should be inspected with insight [*vipassitabbā*].’
an4.94:2.7: At a later time, he has both attainment of inward settling of the mind [*ajjhattaṁ cetosamathassa*] and attainment of insight into *dhammas* through higher discernment [*adhipaññādhammavipassanāya*].
an4.94:3.1: There, *bhikkhus*, by the person who has attainment of insight into *dhammas* through higher discernment [*adhipaññādhammavipassanāya*] but not attainment of inward settling of the mind [*ajjhattaṁ cetosamathassa*], the person who has attainment of inward settling of the mind [*ajjhattaṁ cetosamathassa*] should be approached, and he should be spoken to thus:
an4.94:3.2: ‘How, friend, should the mind [*cittaṁ*] be steadied?
an4.94:3.3: How should the mind [*cittaṁ*] be settled down?
an4.94:3.4: How should the mind [*cittaṁ*] be made unified [*ekodi*]?
an4.94:3.5: How should the mind [*cittaṁ*] be brought into *samādhi* [*samādahātabbaṁ*]?’
an4.94:3.6: He answers him according to what he has seen and according to what he has understood:
an4.94:3.7: ‘In this way, friend, the mind [*cittaṁ*] should be steadied; in this way the mind [*cittaṁ*] should be settled down; in this way the mind [*cittaṁ*] should be made unified [*ekodi*]; in this way the mind [*cittaṁ*] should be brought into *samādhi* [*samādahātabbaṁ*].’
an4.94:3.8: At a later time, he has both attainment of insight into *dhammas* through higher discernment [*adhipaññādhammavipassanāya*] and attainment of inward settling of the mind [*ajjhattaṁ cetosamathassa*].
an4.94:4.1: There, *bhikkhus*, by the person who has neither attainment of inward settling of the mind [*ajjhattaṁ cetosamathassa*], nor attainment of insight into *dhammas* through higher discernment [*adhipaññādhammavipassanāya*], the person who has both attainment of inward settling of the mind [*ajjhattaṁ cetosamathassa*] and attainment of insight into *dhammas* through higher discernment [*adhipaññādhammavipassanāya*] should be approached, and he should be spoken to thus:
an4.94:4.10: ‘In this way, friend, the mind [*cittaṁ*] should be steadied; in this way the mind [*cittaṁ*] should be settled down; in this way the mind [*cittaṁ*] should be made unified [*ekodi*]; in this way the mind [*cittaṁ*] should be brought into *samādhi* [*samādahātabbaṁ*]; in this way *saṅkhāras* should be seen; in this way *saṅkhāras* should be examined; in this way *saṅkhāras* should be inspected with insight [*vipassitabbā*].’
an4.94:4.11: At a later time, he has both attainment of inward settling of the mind [*ajjhattaṁ cetosamathassa*] and attainment of insight into *dhammas* through higher discernment [*adhipaññādhammavipassanāya*].
an4.94:4.2: ‘How, friend, should the mind [*cittaṁ*] be steadied?
an4.94:4.3: How should the mind [*cittaṁ*] be settled down?
an4.94:4.4: How should the mind [*cittaṁ*] be made unified [*ekodi*]?
an4.94:4.5: How should the mind [*cittaṁ*] be brought into *samādhi* [*samādahātabbaṁ*]?
an4.94:4.8: How should *saṅkhāras* be inspected with insight [*vipassitabbā*]?’
an4.94:4.9: He answers him according to what he has seen and according to what he has understood:
an4.94:5.1: There, *bhikkhus*, by the person who has both attainment of inward settling of the mind [*ajjhattaṁ cetosamathassa*] and attainment of insight into *dhammas* through higher discernment [*adhipaññādhammavipassanāya*], standing firm in those wholesome *dhammas*, further effort should be made for the destruction of the *āsavas*.
an4.94:5.2: These four persons, *bhikkhus*, are found existing in the world.”
